title: C3 shared compliance module + C2 parse-once in orchestrator + data.ts comment
acceptance_criteria: Create src/lib/scanner/compliance-shared.ts exporting the single source of truth: deriveComplianceStatus(finding) -> 'observed'|'not-observed'|'not-evaluated', the FAIL_CLOSED_KEYWORDS list, MODULE_FRAMEWORKS map, and FRAMEWORK_ORDER. Logic must be byte-equivalent to the current compliance.ts derivation (no behaviour change)
Refactor compliance.ts to import and use compliance-shared.ts (delete its local copies of the rule/map/order)

C2: in runComplianceModules, parse the chosen HTML (cleanedHtml ?? renderedHtml ?? html) with cheerio EXACTLY ONCE, set it on the ctx passed to the modules as ctx.dom (the optional field added by FIX-01). Guard for empty/absent html (do not pass a dom built from undefined). No behaviour change vs per-module parsing
Fix the stale 'No runtime wiring yet' comment in data.ts (wiring exists in index.ts)
Orchestrator stays pure (no outbound request). `npm run lint` clean; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run test` passes
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
